title: gladiolus segetum subsp. commutatus
intro: Gladiolus segetum subsp. commutatus, also known as the wild gladiolus or meadow gladiolus, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Iridaceae family and is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and corms can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that offer health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and protect the body from damage. Additionally, the plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies.
